topic: An athlete doesn't make the team they expected to join
raw_output:
[story 1]

The acceptance letter felt like a misplaced bookmark in a very important book. Liam stared at the glossy paper, the ink blurring slightly under his gaze. He’d poured everything into those early morning runs, the grueling weight sessions, the relentless dedication. He’d visualized this moment—the roar of the crowd, the handshake of the coach, the shared triumph. Instead, the email simply stated he hadn't been selected for the varsity squad. A slow, heavy feeling settled in his chest, a dull ache that spread outward. He picked up his phone, the screen illuminating a landscape of unread messages, his thumb hovering over the contact for his teammate, Maya. He didn't know how to process the sudden drop in his stomach, the way his hands started to tremble slightly.

[story 2]

"No," I whispered, the sound catching in my throat. I reread the official notification three times, the words swimming before my eyes. It was the third year I’d chased this specific spot, the one I’d mapped out in my head since I was twelve. I walked out of the gymnasium, the echoes of practice suddenly sounding hollow and mocking. My shoulders slumped, a profound weight pressing down on my spine. I kept bumping into lockers, my gaze fixed on the scuffed linoleum floor. Every movement felt sluggish, as if the air itself had thickened. I needed an explanation, a logical sequence to make sense of this deviation from the expected path. I turned, intending to seek out Coach Miller, but the path to his office seemed impossibly long.

[story 3]

The cheers of the other hopefuls seemed to bounce off the walls of the stadium, sharp and distant. I stood near the edge of the field, watching the team warm up, a strange detachment washing over me. My muscles felt strangely inert, heavy with an unspent energy. I watched the designated starters jog onto the field, their confidence radiating outward, a warmth I couldn't seem to access. My jaw tightened, a small, involuntary clench. *Why?* The question echoed in the sudden quiet inside my head. I watched a familiar figure jog past—a teammate I hadn't spoken to much this season—and felt a strange disconnect, as if I were observing a scene from a movie I wasn't supposed to be part of.
